mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or signal Sharpe ratio is negative over 5-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization tightening that supports margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained employment growth that supports aggregate consumption and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 10-year sample, or positive payroll months yield negative returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels breaching the 90th percentile of their 5-year distribution trigger aggressive monetary tightening expectations that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 90th percentile of trailing 5-year distribution
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of threshold-crossing events, or inflation percentile signal loses predictive significance after controlling for term premium changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales where retail sales outpace GDP indicates accelerating consumer spending that drives immediate equity repricing, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS outperformance relative to GDPC1 fails to generate positive 20-day returns in >50% of quarters, or interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that force earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during post-shock periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or signal accuracy drops below 45% in walk-forward validation.
